name: Valdecañas Reservoir Loop
name_original: El Puente del Arzobispo desde Bohonal de Ibor – circular por el Embalse de Valdecañas
description: Cycle a difficult 53.3-mile (85.7 km) route around Embalse de Valdecañas, exploring historical sites and diverse Extremaduran landscapes.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Bohonal De Ibor features routes through varied landscapes, including river valleys and reservoir areas. The region is characterized by its connection to the Río Tajo and the Valdecañas Reservoir, offering routes with water views and open terrain. Cyclists can expect a mix of flat sections and moderate climbs, with some routes passing by historical points of interest like Monroy Castle. The area provides a diverse backdrop for touring cyclists seeking different levels of challenge. Best touring cycling routes around Bohonal De Ibor The most popular touring cycling route is Bohonal de Ibor from Belvís de Monroy – circular by Río Tajo, a 26.8 miles (43.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 50 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive views along the Río Tajo. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Monroy Castle Loop from Navalmoral de la Mata, a moderate 20.1 miles (32.3 km) path. This route circles the historic Monroy Castle, providing a mix of cultural interest and open landscapes. Local touring cyclists also love the Valdecañas Reservoir Loop, a 53.3 miles (85.7 km) trail leading through reservoir landscapes, often completed in about 5 hours 43 minutes. Touring cycling around Bohonal De Ibor is defined by river valleys, reservoir views, and historical sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more advanced bike trails Bohonal De Ibor. The routes in Bohonal De Ibor are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 40 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Bohonal De Ibor's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Bohonal De Ibor?
A: There are 5 touring cycling routes documented around Bohonal De Ibor on komoot, offering diverse experiences through river valleys and reservoir areas.

Q: What are the typical distances for touring cycling routes in Bohonal De Ibor?
A: The touring cycling routes in Bohonal De Ibor vary in length. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111904297/monroy-castle-loop-from-navalmoral-de-la-mata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monroy Castle Loop from Navalmoral de la Mata&lt;/a&gt; is about 32.3 km (20.1 miles), while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111948516/valdecanas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdecañas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; extends to 85.7 km (53.3 miles).

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes in Bohonal De Ibor?
A: Yes, Bohonal De Ibor offers several challenging routes. Two of the five available routes are rated as difficult, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111890518/bohonal-de-ibor-from-belvis-de-monroy-circular-by-rio-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bohonal de Ibor from Belvís de Monroy – circular by Río Tajo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111948516/valdecanas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdecañas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Bohonal De Ibor?
A: The touring cycling routes currently listed for Bohonal De Ibor on komoot are rated as moderate or difficult. There are no routes specifically categorized as easy for beginners, so some cycling experience is recommended.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Bohonal De Ibor?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Bohonal De Ibor is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer.

Q: What kind of views can I expect along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer extensive views, particularly along the Río Tajo and around the Valdecañas Reservoir. You can expect open landscapes, water views, and glimpses of historical sites.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes in Bohonal De Ibor?
A: Yes, several touring cycling routes in the area are circular. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111890518/bohonal-de-ibor-from-belvis-de-monroy-circular-by-rio-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bohonal de Ibor from Belvís de Monroy – circular by Río Tajo&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111904297/monroy-castle-loop-from-navalmoral-de-la-mata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monroy Castle Loop from Navalmoral de la Mata&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111948516/valdecanas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdecañas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in history. Along your rides, you might encounter sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5692387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Temple of the Woodcutters of Augustobriga&lt;/a&gt;, the ruins of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talavera la Vieja&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5583007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Belvís de Monroy&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111904297/monroy-castle-loop-from-navalmoral-de-la-mata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monroy Castle Loop from Navalmoral de la Mata&lt;/a&gt; specifically circles the historic Monroy Castle.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Bohonal De Ibor?
A: The touring cycling routes around Bohonal De Ibor are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Cyclists often appreciate the varied landscapes, including river valleys and reservoir views, as well as the historical points of interest along the way.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Valdecañas Reservoir?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111948516/valdecanas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdecañas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent route that leads through the reservoir landscapes, offering extensive water views and open terrain.

Q: What should I wear for touring cycling in Bohonal De Ibor during summer?
A: During summer, temperatures in Bohonal De Ibor can be quite high. It's advisable to wear lightweight, breathable cycling apparel, use sunscreen, and carry plenty of water. Starting your rides early in the morning can help avoid the midday heat.

Q: Where can I find parking for cycling routes in Bohonal De Ibor?
A: While specific parking areas aren't detailed for each route, towns like Navalmoral de la Mata, which serve as starting points for some tours, typically offer public parking options. It's recommended to check local parking availability when planning your trip.
tips: Bohonal de Ibor is a small town near the Valdecañas reservoir and the Tagus river. Stop at Plaza España to eat something and have a coffee and admire the Church of San Bartolomé Apóstol.
Remains of a marble temple from an ancient Roman city called Augustobriga. Spectacular views of the Valdecañas reservoir and the bridge that crosses Guadalupe.
Bridge over the Tagus River and the EX-118 Valdecañas Reservoir road between Bohonal de Ibor and Peraleda de la Mata. You can't walk past without stopping for a photo.

The Archbishop's Bridge is a magnificent example of medieval engineering. The bridge was built to allow trade and pilgrims to cross the Tagus River in 1380, but was further widened in the 18th century to allow passage after floods.

Built at the end of the 14th century, the bridge has given its name to the town on the other side of the Tagus. It plays a fundamental role in communication with Extremadura, Ávila and Lisbon. Going a little further along the banks of the river, you will have some beautiful views of the bridge with the town behind.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: This challenging touring bicycle route is approximately 85.7 kilometers long with 940 meters of elevation gain. It typically takes around 5 hours and 42 minutes to complete, but this can vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or points of interest.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is categorized as difficult and requires very good physical condition. With 85.7 kilometers and 940 meters of elevation gain, it's best suited for experienced touring cyclists. Some sections may even require pushing your bike due to challenging terrain.

faqs.question: Where can I start this circular touring bicycle route and find parking?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Bohonal de Ibor, as indicated in its name. You should be able to find suitable parking within the village to begin your circular journey.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and contributes to the route's difficult rating. It follows historical paths like the Camino Real de Guadalupe, which suggests a mix of paved and unpaved or less-maintained sections. You'll cycle through diverse landscapes including forests, crop fields, olive and almond groves, and holm oak woodlands.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a rich blend of natural beauty and historical sites. You'll pass by the Church of San Bartolomé Apóstol in Bohonal de Ibor, the Temple of the Woodcutters of Augustobriga, and the impressive Valdecañas Reservoir Bridge, which is an excellent spot for photographs. The historic settlement of El Puente del Arzobispo and its magnificent medieval bridge (Puente del Arzobispo) are also key highlights. The expansive Embalse de Valdecañas and the Tagus River provide stunning natural backdrops.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while cycling this route?
faqs.answer: The diverse landscapes of Extremadura, including olive and almond trees, holm oaks, and open crop fields, provide habitats for various wildlife. You might spot local species such as roe deer and partridges during your ride.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While the route can be enjoyed in various seasons, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with milder temperatures. Summers in Extremadura can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable if cycling during that season.

faqs.question: Can I bring my dog on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: Komoot generally recommends checking local regulations for specific trails regarding dogs. Given the length and difficulty of this touring bicycle route, and the potential for varied terrain and wildlife encounters, it's advisable to ensure your dog is well-conditioned for such a ride and to keep them on a leash for their safety and that of local wildlife.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: This route traverses the Villuercas-Ibores-Jara Geopark. Generally, there are no specific entrance fees or permits required for cycling on public trails within the Geopark. However, always respect local signage and regulations, especially concerning protected areas or private land.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for a touring bicycle trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, wear appropriate cycling attire, including a helmet, padded shorts, and layers suitable for changing weather conditions. Bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bicycle, a first-aid kit, and a fully charged phone with the Komoot app or a GPS device. Sun protection (sunscreen, sunglasses) is also highly recommended.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route overlaps with several other notable paths. You'll find sections that are part of the Ciclosenda del Tajo, the River Tajo Nature Trail, and historical routes such as the Camino Real de Guadalupe. Other intersecting trails include La Jara and Camino de los Jerónimos.
